message:
fix(consumer): prune expired revocation entries to prevent unbounded growth

FabricConsumerCursor::revoked_tickets grew without bound because
revoke_read_ticket() inserted handles but nothing ever removed them.
After a ticket's TTL expires, the expiry check in validate_ticket()
already rejects it, making the revocation entry redundant.

Changes:
- revoked_tickets: BTreeSet -> BTreeMap<Handle, not_after_tick>
- revoke_read_ticket() now takes not_after_tick parameter
- Added prune_expired_revocations() to remove entries past their TTL
- Added regression: prune_expired_revocations_removes_stale_entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
